title: Hongdae after dark
body:
The university district around Hongik is Seoul's youth-and-nightlife core — street buskers, arcade halls, themed cafés, late-night eats, and anime goods. It's the natural evening base for nights you're not at the arena. Completely foreigner-accessible: no Korean contacts needed, no documented door discrimination (unlike parts of Gangnam), and most restaurants stay open until 04:00.

Two bars worth knowing — both walkable from Hongik Univ. Station:

  • Mike's Cabin — 94-13 Eoulmadang-ro, Mapo-gu (~5-min walk from Exit 8). Open Mon–Thu & Sun 18:00–02:00, Fri–Sat 18:00–05:00 (+82-2-326-1921). The go-to foreigner bar in Hongdae: reggaeton, pop, hip-hop, mixed local and international crowd. Cover ≈₩10,000 on weekends (⚠ confirm at door — covers move). Arrive before 22:30 to beat the queue.
  • Dokkaebi Party House — 22 Wausan-ro 17-gil, Seogyo-dong, Mapo-gu (~7-min walk from Hongik Univ. Station). One of Hongdae's biggest foreigner-friendly clubs: EDM to hip-hop, large mixed crowd. Fast-track entry + 1 free drink available via Klook in advance (⚠ verify cover on Klook before the trip).

Clubs fill at midnight and peak 01:00–03:00. Tuesday Jul 14 is the best night of this trip to stay late — no early alarm on Wednesday, and the flight home isn't until 21:30.

title: PC bangs, arcades & LoL Park
body: Korea's PC bangs — high-spec gaming cafés charging by the hour, often with food service — are everywhere and cheap; they're a rite of passage. Arcades lean into rhythm and crane games. League fans should check the LCK schedule: LoL Park in Jongno is the league's home studio/arena, and the Summer split resumes after MSI, so a live LCK match in Seoul may be on while you're there.

title: Norebang, Han River & the ones Americans miss
body:
Norebang (노래방) — private karaoke
Probably the most fun 2–3 hours in Seoul for a group. You rent a private room by the hour — typically ≈₩15,000–25,000/hour for a group room (⚠ verify at the venue) — stock it with drinks from the lobby vending machine, and sing on a proper AV system with a catalogue of thousands including English titles. Completely private: no audience, no judgment, no one else watching. Hongdae is dense with noraebangs; look for neon signs or search Naver Map for 노래방. Walk-in is usually fine; late Friday and Saturday nights fill up, so arrive before midnight. Best fit for this trip: Tuesday evening (Jul 14) after the Secret Garden, since Wednesday's flight is in the evening and there's no early alarm.

Han River parks
Summer evenings, locals grab fried chicken from a nearby spot and eat on the grass by the river. Yeouido Hangang Park (Line 5, Yeouido Station, Exit 2–3) and Banpo Hangang Park (taxi ≈₩8,000–12,000 from Hongdae, or Line 2 to Dangsan then Line 9 to National Assembly (~20 min)) are the main ones. Bike rental available at both. The Banpo Bridge Rainbow Fountain runs free light-and-water shows daily at set times in summer (typically several showtimes between 19:00 and 22:00 — ⚠ verify the current schedule on Naver Map or at the bridge before going). No entry fee, no language needed. A natural warm-evening option any night you're back in Hongdae with energy to spare — it's a short hop from Yeouido. Note Monday Jul 13 you'll be returning late from the Busan day trip (~22:00), so it fits better earlier in the week.

Korean corn dog (gamja hot dog)
A potato-battered hot dog — often with mozzarella inside, sometimes rolled in fries, dipped in your choice of sauces. Every street food zone in Myeongdong and Hongdae has them. ≈₩2,500–4,500 per piece (⚠ check the stall). Messy and worth it.

Makgeolli (막걸리)
Creamy, lightly fizzy unfiltered rice wine; lower alcohol than soju and considerably less harsh. At a sit-down spot a ceramic jug is typically ≈₩8,000–15,000 (⚠ verify). Pairs specifically with pajeon (scallion pancake) — Koreans associate the combination with rainy monsoon evenings, and after one July shower in Seoul you'll feel why immediately. Insadong has traditional makgeolli spots built into its alleys; Yeonnam-dong (walkable from Hongik Univ. Station Exit 3, ~5 min) has a more modern bar take.

Daiso
There are branches across Seoul including near Hongdae (search 'Daiso 홍대' on Naver Map). The Korean equivalent of a dollar store but actually good: travel accessories, skincare gadgets, character goods, snacks, phone cases — most things ₩1,000–5,000. Worth 30 minutes for cheap souvenirs and any trip supplies you realise you need. Completely self-service; no language required.

title: Anime & game shopping
body:
Animate Seoul (5F, AK& Mall, 188 Yanghwa-ro, Mapo-gu — Hongdae Station Exit 9; open ~11:00–22:00) is the largest anime specialist in the Hongdae area: figures, manga, Blu-ray, merchandise, and rotating pop-ups for seasonal titles. Yongsan i'Park Mall electronics floors carry games, trading cards, and figures — verify the current tenant mix at the mall, as renovations have shifted some vendors. KWANGYA@Seoul (SM's official store — B1, D-Tower, Seongsu; Ttukseom Station Exit 5, Line 2; ~10:30–20:00) has SM artists' albums, photocards, and a media zone. (The old SMTOWN COEX Artium closed in 2020.) Myeongdong K-pop street around Myeongdong Station has comprehensive stock across all agencies; go early or skip if the weekend crowds are too much.

HYBE — the Yongsan HYBE Insight museum has closed; HYBE now runs artist merch as rotating pop-ups (often AK Plaza Hongdae or near the Yongsan HQ). Check the Weverse Shop and HYBE socials the week before for what's live. LoL Park (3F Gran Seoul Building, 33 Jong-ro, Jongno-gu; open 11:00–20:00; Jonggak Station Exit 1) is the LCK's home venue with a free-to-browse exhibition floor, Riot merch store, and PC café. LCK Summer split resumes after MSI — check the schedule for a live match while you're there.
